records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Disabused (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Disabusing.]

defs: To set free from mistakes; to undeceive; to disengage from fallacy or deception; to set right; -- often used with of; as, to disabuse one of his illusions.
quotes: To undeceive and disabuse the people.
If men are now sufficiently enlightened to disabuse themselves or artifice, hypocrisy, and superstition, they will consider this event as an era in their history.
